body.text: The dam breaks: en banc Third Circuit strikes down New Jersey's AR-15 and magazine bans 10–5 — the first circuit ever to invalidate a state "assault weapon" law

In ANJRPC v. AG of New Jersey (192 pp., cross-party majority), the court held AR-15s and 10+ round magazines are "plainly in common use" and constitutionally protected. The ruling creates a direct circuit split just weeks before SCOTUS hears Viramontes in October.

facts.title: 1. Third Circuit strikes down NJ AR-15 & magazine bans
2. Virginia — T-1: injunction takes effect July 21
3. SCOTUS AR-15 case — summer order list today

body.text:
• VA files Crump v. Katz motion: "AR-15s aren't protected arms" + interest balancing
• DOJ abandons post-office carry-ban appeal; SAF/FPC injunction stands
• SCOTUS summer order list releases 9:30 AM ET today — check held petitions
• NJ sues JSD Supply founder personally under public-nuisance law
